metadata.title: States PCS: Tamil Nadu GK Questions for TNPSC Examinations

questions.question: Which ancient port city in Tamil Nadu was famously known as the gateway to Roman trade?
questions.explanation: Puhar, also called Kaveripattinam, was a major Chola port and center for Indo-Roman maritime trade, evidenced by Roman artifacts found there.

questions.question: Which Pallava ruler is credited with introducing rock-cut architecture at Mahabalipuram?
questions.explanation: Mahendravarman I pioneered rock-cut cave temples at Mahabalipuram, marking the beginning of Pallava rock-cut architecture.

questions.question: Which Pandya king reasserted Tamil dominance by defeating the Hoysalas and Cholas in the 13th century?
questions.explanation: Jatavarman Sundara Pandya I defeated the Hoysalas and Cholas, restoring Pandya supremacy and fostering prosperity in 13th-century Tamil Nadu.

questions.question: Which Tamil saint is renowned for composing the devotional hymns collectively known as the “Tiruvachakam”?
questions.explanation: Manikkavacakar, a prominent Tamil Shaivite saint, composed the “Tiruvachakam,” a revered set of hymns dedicated to Lord Shiva.

questions.question: Which Nayak ruler is credited with commissioning the major construction of the Meenakshi Temple complex in Madurai?
questions.explanation: Tirumala Nayak, a 17th-century Madurai Nayak ruler, expanded and renovated the Meenakshi Temple, adding grand gopurams and mandapams.

questions.question: Who led the Salt Satyagraha at Vedaranyam in Tamil Nadu in 1930?
questions.explanation: C. Rajagopalachari led the Vedaranyam Salt Satyagraha in Tamil Nadu in 1930, inspired by Gandhi’s Dandi March.

questions.question: Which archaeological site in Tamil Nadu is renowned for its Iron Age urn burials and ancient graffiti marks?
questions.explanation: Adichanallur in Thoothukudi district is notable for Iron Age urn burials, megalithic remains, and graffiti, with significant finds of Tamil-Brahmi inscriptions and advanced metallurgy.

questions.question: Which mountain pass serves as a major corridor connecting Tamil Nadu and Kerala through the Western Ghats?
questions.explanation: The Palghat Gap is a prominent low pass in the Western Ghats that links Tamil Nadu and Kerala, impacting transport, climate, and monsoon rainfall patterns in the region.

questions.question: Which type of soil predominantly covers the black cotton growing regions of Tamil Nadu?
questions.explanation: Regur, or black soil, rich in minerals and moisture-retentive, is widespread in Tamil Nadu’s cotton regions like Coimbatore and Erode.

questions.question: Which river in Tamil Nadu flows entirely within the state and empties into the Bay of Bengal near Cuddalore?
questions.explanation: The Vellar River originates in the Shevaroy Hills, flows entirely within Tamil Nadu, and joins the Bay of Bengal near Cuddalore (Parangipettai).
